title: Does Lilith actually er talk in EoE
display_title: Does Lilith actually er talk in EoE
wikitext:
[]: Does Lilith actually, er, "talk" in EoE?             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 02, 2008 11:32 am Quick question: I was rewatching End of Eva and its the part where Rei 3 gets absorbed back into Lilith, thus the soul of Lilith contained inside Rei is being reunited with her real body. So the screen flashes a title card that says "Welcome home" Did that....er....actually happen? I mean audibly? Was it nonverbal communication between Rei and Lilith? Or, seeing as it was a title card, was it just some wacky surrealist gimmick?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Mar 02, 2008 12:17 pm I had assumed it was sort of telepathic.

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Sun Mar 02, 2008 12:23 pm Well I had taken it to be some non-verbal communica between Lilith and Rei. Sort of like a "welcome back to me, me." I highly doubt that Gendo could hear it though. Seeing as how Lilith never moved before it seems unlikely that she would do so now before her soul has returned (this was right before Rei was absorbed right? it's been a while).

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 02, 2008 12:49 pm yeah I always assumed it was telepathic too

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sun Mar 02, 2008 1:19 pm How could Lilith communicate with Rei when Rei has Lilith's soul? Since Lilith had no soul, she couldn't have had any consciousness to speak. Plus, if Rei and Lilith were communicating with one another in their minds, why did Rei her words outloud and Lilith didn't? If Rei could have easily said, "I'm home," in her mind, why did she have to say it outloud? I think Rei was saying, "I'm home," to herself. The title card was meant to add another layer of Rei finally returing to Lilith. I don't think the title card was meant to be taken seriously as an actual retort from Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 02, 2008 2:17 pm You make a very good point The Goose. Hmm....

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Mar 02, 2008 2:29 pm The Goose wrote: How could Lilith communicate with Rei when Rei has Lilith's soul? Since Lilith had no soul, she couldn't have had any consciousness to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Sun Mar 02, 2008 3:19 pm I think the main sticking point of this discussion is going to be, can the vessel function without the soul? As the soul was never truly defined in the series except as something that can be manipulated to a limited extent the question exists as what exactly it does for the body. It generates the AT field which provides shape yes but the body must do some of this on its own. The Rei clones had shape yet no soul. Lilith had shape yet (presumably) no soul. Adam embryo had shape yet again no soul. So obviously the ATF isn't all it's cracked up to be in regards to shapeyness. However the soul also functions as the "mind" or at least some aspect of it. As Kyoko doesn't stay sane without all of hers and Rei is slightly (read VERY) off without all of hers. Granted Rei could just be because she is in actually not truly the same as the rest of us but Kaworu seemed at least somewhat normal and he was in the same position. So we come back to what exactly the soul does for the body besides provide sane mental faculties. Is it that Lilith still retained some semblance of "awareness" despite the loss of her soul or is it possible that her body actually had a small soul segment in it still? I mention the second because well let's face it souls are sticky things even inside NGE and it doesn't really seem like they have a total grasp of the manipulation. Plus Lilith's soul was like the second one that they ever tried to manipulate so it really was in the first stages then. Who knows, maybe that's the reason Rei II is so weird: she is actually only in possession of 1/3 her soul rather than 1/2?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Mar 02, 2008 3:33 pm maybe we're reading into this too much and it wasn't even "communication" as in the word "I'm home" so much as just the "mental feeling" "I'm home"...if you follow me. Even without this dialogue the question does get posed: how does Lilith's body even have "form" if according to them everything without a soul cannot generate an A.T. Field (itself a projection of the soul) and thus such a soulless body would revert to Tang. I think the body must just do some of it on its own, but Lilith is pretty "inert" until she gets her soul back.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Mar 02, 2008 3:36 pm Maybe there is a part of the soul that is still present in Lilith that is communicating telepathically?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Mar 02, 2008 3:39 pm The Goose wrote: How could Lilith communicate with Rei when Rei has Lilith's soul? Since Lilith had no soul, she couldn't have had any consciousness to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:12 pm TemporalKnight wrote: I think the main sticking point of this discussion is going to be, can the vessel function without the soul? As the soul was never truly defined in the series except as something that can be manipulated to a limited extent the question exists as what exactly it does for the body. It generates the AT field which provides shape yes but the body must do some of this on its own. The Rei clones had shape yet no soul. Lilith had shape yet (presumably) no soul. Adam embryo had shape yet again no soul. So obviously the ATF isn't all it's cracked up to be in regards to shapeyness.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:15 pm Could the title card be Rei's subconsciousness feeling "at home"?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:16 pm TemporalKnight wrote: As the soul was never truly defined in the series except as something that can be manipulated to a limited extent

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Mar 02, 2008 4:56 pm The title card could just be the same as most of the other titles cards in the series. Though some of them were direct communication, some of the other cards weren't . Such as in Death. So it could just be symbolism of Rei returning to Lilith and finally being "home" again.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 02, 2008 5:04 pm The "Okaerinasai" card is perhaps most comparable, style-wise, to the ones in EoTV that actually "participate" in the internal conversations. That still leaves the problem of what to make of it, though, since it's employed before we enter Instrumentality's anything-goes mindfuck territory...

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Sun Mar 02, 2008 5:10 pm Reichu wrote: RITSUKO: That which can be called Shinji's life still exists. ... IBUKI: We can surmise that Shinji's body lost its ego border, and he's floating in the entry plug in quantum form. Misato: In other words, Shinji has shifted into a form we can't see? IBUKI: Yes, the composition of the LCL inside the plug has chemically altered into something very similar to the sea water of primitive Earth. Misato: A soup of life, huh? RITSUKO: All of the substances which composed Shinji are still preserved in the plug, and what could be called his soul exists there as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Mar 02, 2008 5:11 pm Pre- MindFuck craziness? Well in the series, such as Shinji's personal hell train while in Leliel. I assume that the words weren't being said, but it was direct contact to his brain. Another theory I had, which seems to have been long dismissed, was that it was actually himself speaking to himself. Like his subconscious was reaching out. Idk.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Mar 02, 2008 11:08 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: The title card could just be the same as most of the other titles cards in the series. Though some of them were direct communication, some of the other cards weren't . Such as in Death. So it could just be symbolism of Rei returning to Lilith and finally being "home" again.

[]:             Quote Post by Jumpstart A. Cat » Tue Mar 04, 2008 9:31 pm Reichu wrote: What does she mean by "quantum form", anyway?
